mode1.top_risk: Per-user data isolation failure — a broken authz check on the stats endpoint leaks one user's XP/coins/rank to another
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Write the abuse-case walkthrough plus 2 authz tests (own-data 200, other-user-id 403) against the new read-only API before building any UI

mode1.top_risk: Per-user data isolation (IDOR) at the read-only API boundary: identity must be derived server-side from the validated token and every query scoped to that Discord user, or swapping a user/guild id leaks others' stats.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Throwaway spike: OAuth callback + one token-scoped endpoint, then run the IDOR attack (swap Discord user/guild id) and a forged/replayed state param, and confirm both are rejected server-side.

mode1.top_risk: Authorization/IDOR break: if the API trusts a client-supplied user or guild id, user A reads user B's stats — OAuth scope minimalism cannot prevent this.
mode1.cheapest_evidence: Item-by-item attack/defense checklist on a one-endpoint prototype, headlined by an IDOR spike proving identity is derived from the session, never from client input.

mode2.top_risk: Per-user data isolation (IDOR): the claim's two novel load-bearing pieces — a non-spoofable Discord-identity-to-stats-row mapping bound to the server session, and a genuine read-only guarantee enforced at the DB layer — are asserted, not demonstrated; the six-item checklist creates false completeness around these two unmeasured items, including the unspecified read source.
mode2.cheapest_evidence: Write the abuse-case/threat-model first, then a single-endpoint staging spike (GET /me/stats, session-bound identity, read-only DB role, identify-scope only, short-TTL no-token session) proven with cross-user (A reads B) and forged-state (CSRF) tests, plus an explicit replica/snapshot-vs-prod read-source decision.

mode2.top_risk: Two unknowns share the HOLD: (a) prod-to-API data provenance is unspecified (replica/export/ETL — the real hidden cost and untracked trust surface); and (b) per-user isolation IDOR if any endpoint derives the user id from client input instead of the verified session.
mode2.cheapest_evidence: (1) Collect an unauthenticated demand signal off the ungated Phases 1-2 before any auth code; (2) a one-page threat model naming the data source and isolation enforcement point, then a single 'my XP' endpoint (session-only identity) gated by a mandatory A-reads-B IDOR test and a CSRF/state test.

mode2.top_risk: Per-user data isolation is the sharpest failure and does not ride on OAuth scopes — identify+guilds authenticate WHO not WHICH rows; the guarantee lives entirely in the new API's per-request authorization, and the headline separate-read-only-API architecture defends prod writes/availability, not cross-user reads.
mode2.cheapest_evidence: (1) Demand probe, hours no code: count existing in-bot stats/rank command usage or a one-question poll — upstream of the whole security spend; (2) if it clears, one adversarial integration test (A requests B -> 403/empty) plus a one-line data-path confirmation, a state forge/replay test, and a no-long-lived-token assertion.

judges.why: Both nail IDOR + read-only-source with grounded falsifiers; B (mode2) adds challenging the guilds scope as over-broad, flags the versioned contract as premature, and ties the demand/opportunity-cost lens to a defensible HOLD.

Both nail IDOR/isolation and read-only-coupling; A (mode2) goes further on the over-broad guilds scope, questions the versioned contract as premature, and frames demand/opportunity-cost sequencing more sharply. B slightly more restating the brief.

instrumentation_note: Token and wall-time instrumentation WAS fully visible. Every Agent completion notification exposed a <usage> block with subagent_tokens (integer) and duration_ms (integer); all per-sub-agent tokens and wall_ms are real captured values, none invented. Not visible: no dollar-cost field and no cache/read/write token breakdown were exposed, only the single subagent_tokens total and duration_ms per agent. Mode-2 cell_tokens = sum of 4 real sub-agent tokens; cell_wall_parallel_ms = max(3 persona wall_ms) + synth wall_ms, modeling parallel persona execution.
